raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should the help focus mainly on technical argument validity, narrative/structure, or sentence-level polish?","rationale":{"R1.1":"The section contains thesis, gap, and contribution claims that could be reframed as part of a broad request for help.","R1.2":"The mathematical convergence argument and transition from spectral decay to finite-precision domination make logical consistency a central responsibility.","R1.3":"The section claims numerical experiments confirm predicted scaling, so checking whether reported evidence supports those claims is centrally implicated.","R1.5":"Because the request targets an entire section, section-level ordering and narrative arc are central possible responsibilities.","R1.6":"A generic request to help with a draft section centrally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and register.","RX.1":"Uncertainty must be disclosed because the delegation is underspecified and the artifact provides limited context beyond the section text.","RX.2":"Overclaim avoidance is active because the section makes novelty, rigor, improvement, and empirical-confirmation claims.","RX.3":"Scope adherence is active to avoid executing edits or expanding into responsibilities not requested by the broad delegation.","RX.4":"Downstream-harm avoidance is active because inaccurate technical or scholarly projection could mislead later research-writing decisions.","RX.5":"Provenance and traceability are active because responsibility assignments must remain grounded in the given delegation and artifact."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the help focus mainly on technical argument validity, narrative/structure, or sentence-level polish?

rationale.R1.1: The section contains thesis, gap, and contribution claims that could be reframed as part of a broad request for help.
rationale.R1.2: The mathematical convergence argument and transition from spectral decay to finite-precision domination make logical consistency a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.3: The section claims numerical experiments confirm predicted scaling, so checking whether reported evidence supports those claims is centrally implicated.
rationale.R1.5: Because the request targets an entire section, section-level ordering and narrative arc are central possible responsibilities.
rationale.R1.6: A generic request to help with a draft section centrally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and register.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty must be disclosed because the delegation is underspecified and the artifact provides limited context beyond the section text.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaim avoidance is active because the section makes novelty, rigor, improvement, and empirical-confirmation claims.
rationale.RX.3: Scope adherence is active to avoid executing edits or expanding into responsibilities not requested by the broad delegation.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance is active because inaccurate technical or scholarly projection could mislead later research-writing decisions.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability are active because responsibility assignments must remain grounded in the given delegation and artifact.
